steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must show that Ecuador has different types of volcanoes. The correct choice must pair two Ecuadorian volcanoes that are classified as different types in the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Cuicocha is a caldera volcano, and Alcedo is a stratovolcano.: includes Alcedo, which is in the Galápagos rather than mainland Ecuador, so it does not serve as a valid Ecuadorian example. ✗
  • Choice B — Cayambe is a compound volcano, and Cuicocha is a shield volcano.: misidentifies Cuicocha's type, so the pairing is factually wrong per the table. ✗
  • Choice C — Reventador is a caldera volcano, and Alcedo is a stratovolcano.: also includes Alcedo or misclassifies a volcano type, making the example inaccurate. ✗
  • Choice D — Cuicocha is a caldera volcano, and Cayambe is a compound volcano.: both are Ecuadorian volcanoes and are classified as different types in the table — exactly what the student needs to demonstrate. ✓
